I'll search for this reference to verify the metadata and assess whether it's hallucinated.
Based on my web search results, I have found the actual reference and can verify its metadata.

The GitHub repository at https://github.com/abhinand5/MedEmbed contains official citation information that shows the correct author format and title. 
The official citation for this software is: "@software{balachandran2024medembed, author = {Balachandran, Abhinand}, title = {MedEmbed: Medical-Focused Embedding Models}, year = {2024}, url = {https://github.com/abhinand5/MedEmbed}}"


The cited reference uses "Abhinand Balachandran" as the author, which matches the full name format shown in the official citation (Balachandran, Abhinand). The username "abhinand5" is simply the GitHub account username, not the actual author name. The automated checker's report of an "author mismatch" between the formal name "Abhinand Balachandran" and the GitHub username "abhinand5" is a misidentification—the checker appears to have conflated the GitHub username with the author name field. The actual author name used in official citations and documentation is "Abhinand Balachandran," which matches the cited reference.

EXPLANATION: The reference is to a real software project with an exact title and author match in official documentation. The automated checker's "mismatch" between "Abhinand Balachandran" and "abhinand5" reflects a confusion between the GitHub username and the formal author name.
